pi_items.source: setup_task.sh embedded usa.gdt file variable definition
task.json metadata verified against task description. gdp_growth is created from gdp using formula: series gdp_growth = 100 * diff(log(gdp)). inf is in the dataset.

pi_items.verified_value: series gdp_growth = 100 * diff(log(gdp)) calculates quarterly percentage growth rate
pi_items.source: task.json task description and web search confirming this is standard econometric formula for log-difference percentage growth
task.json metadata and task description specify: var 4 gdp_growth inf

Web search confirmed these are standard components of VAR (Vector Autoregression) analysis with Granger causality tests. VAR system produces coefficient estimates; F-tests or Wald tests test Granger causality; p-values report statistical significance.
VAR model with 2 variables (gdp_growth, inf) produces 2 equations with coefficient estimates for each variable and its lags
Web search confirmed VAR output includes F-test statistics for Granger causality testing (testing whether lagged values of each variable help predict the other)

privileged_info_summary: The usa.gdt dataset is a real quarterly US macroeconomic dataset from the Principles of Econometrics (POE5) textbook collection, containing Real GDP (in billions of chained 2005 dollars) and inflation rate (quarterly % change in CPI, annualized) from Q1 1984 to Q3 2009 with 103 observations. The task requires estimating a 4-lag VAR model on GDP growth (calculated as 100 * diff(log(gdp))) and inflation. VAR output includes coefficient estimates for both equations and F-test statistics for Granger causality tests with p-values for statistical significance testing.
